cards.name: Café San José
cards.address: 1583A Meridian Ave, San Jose, CA 95125, USA
cards.blurb: Start the day with coffee and a bite at Café San José — cozy neighborhood spot, good for settling in before the day picks up.
cards.why: A solid morning anchor before you get moving.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk the Los Gatos Creek Trail and shake off the morning. It's shaded and easy, with the creek running alongside the whole way.
cards.why: Best way to get outside before the rain rolls in heavier later.

cards.name: Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge
cards.address: 37 N Santa Cruz Ave, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Drop into Petroglyph Ceramic Lounge and paint a piece of pottery. No skills required, just pick a piece, grab the paints, and go.
cards.why: Rainy-day weather is basically made for this kind of afternoon.

cards.name: Philharmonia Baroque's Artist Salon--Shunske Sato at Martin Luther King Junior Library
cards.blurb: Catch Philharmonia Baroque's Artist Salon with Shunske Sato at the MLK Jr. Library — free, intimate, and totally worth an hour of your afternoon.
cards.why: Free live music from a world-class baroque violinist is a rare Monday treat.

cards.name: Ritual at Manresa
cards.address: 320 Village Ln, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Ritual at Manresa for a proper lunch. It's a higher-end spot in Los Gatos — worth lingering over.
cards.why: You'll want a real meal after a creative morning, and this one delivers.

cards.name: tUrn Open Reading of Climate Plays: Informal Climate Play-Reading for actors and non-actors
cards.blurb: Join the tUrn Open Reading of Climate Plays at Santa Clara University — part performance, part conversation, and open to anyone whether you want to read or just listen.
cards.why: A genuinely interesting way to spend a Monday evening without spending a dime.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Walk or bike the Los Gatos Creek Trail with the kids. It's flat, shaded, and follows the creek the whole way — easy going for little legs.
cards.why: A relaxed morning outside before the rain picks up is a smart call today.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Main Street Cupertino and grab lunch at Lazy Dog or one of the Asian spots along the plaza. The outdoor walkway is easy to stroll with kids in tow.
cards.why: Plenty of casual, affordable options under one plaza so everyone can find something they want.

cards.name: Cupertino Library
cards.address: 10800 Torre Ave, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Pop into Cupertino Library for a couple hours. Kids can browse the shelves, grab picture books or chapter books, and there are usually activity stations set up for younger ones.
cards.why: Rainy-day weather calls for a cozy indoor stop and this one is genuinely fun for kids.

cards.name: Katsu
cards.address: 859 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Katsu in Mountain View for a mid-afternoon snack or early plate. Small, cozy spot with food that punches well above its size.
cards.why: Great food worth stopping for on a slow Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Steve Jobs in Exile
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Check out the Steve Jobs in Exile exhibition at the Computer History Museum. It covers the wild years between Apple and NeXT and it's more visually engaging than you'd expect for kids curious about tech.
cards.why: The Computer History Museum is a genuinely great family museum and this exhibit is one of the better ones up right now.

cards.name: Massage Envy
cards.address: 1040 Grant Rd #110, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the afternoon with a relaxing massage or bodywork session — a solid way to decompress after a full day of walking and exploring.
cards.why: You've been on your feet since morning, so a little self-care right in Mountain View is a natural way to close out the day.

cards.name: Downtown Campbell
cards.address: Campbell Ave & E Campbell Ave
cards.blurb: Stroll Campbell Avenue for dinner, drinks, and some window shopping — the compact downtown strip has a good mix of restaurants and casual spots to settle into for the evening.
cards.why: Ending back in your anchor city keeps the evening low-key and lets you wrap up close to home.

cards.name: Brew Bytes Cafe
cards.address: 19998 Homestead Rd Suite C, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Brew Bytes in Cupertino — solid coffee, good breakfast bites, and a chill vibe to ease into the day before the rain picks up.
cards.why: A warm cup and a low-key start are exactly what a cool, rainy Monday calls for.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Take a walk along Los Gatos Creek Trail. The canopy keeps a lot of the drizzle off and the creek sounds especially good when it's running high from rain.
cards.why: It's one of those spots that's genuinely better on a moody, overcast morning.

cards.name: Philharmonia Baroque's Artist Salon--Shunske Sato at Martin Luther King Junior Library
cards.blurb: Philharmonia Baroque’s Artist Salons are pre-concert talks led by performers, scholars, and guest artists. Offered a few weeks before each concert set, they giv
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Hungry Hound
cards.address: 1000 Lafayette St C, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Hungry Hound right in Santa Clara. Casual, affordable, and solid food to recharge before the afternoon.
cards.why: Quick, tasty, and you're already in the neighborhood.

cards.name: tUrn Climate Science en Español: Glaciares y Deshielo Glaciar | Glaciars & Glacial Melt
cards.blurb: Catch this free bilingual climate science talk on glaciers and glacial melt at SCU. It's a accessible, well-produced lecture and the kind of thing that's easy to walk into and walk away genuinely informed.
cards.why: Two good talks in one afternoon on a rainy Monday is honestly a great use of the day.

cards.name: Svetlana’s Massage
cards.address: 15066 Los Gatos Almaden Rd Ste 120-A, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Svetlana's in Los Gatos and let the afternoon melt off. A 5-star rating means they take this seriously, so you're in good hands.
cards.why: After a full day of walking and sitting through talks, this is the move.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Bundle up and walk the lake loop at Vasona. Kids love spotting ducks along the water, and if the little train is running they'll lose their minds — it's a short, easy loop that stays interesting the whole way around.
cards.why: Light rain won't ruin this one, and kids genuinely love it every single time.

cards.name: Anchors Fish & Chips And Seafood Grill
cards.address: 14441 Big Basin Wy, Saratoga, CA 95070, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Anchors in Saratoga for fish and chips. Order at the counter, grab a basket, and let the kids go to town — it's exactly the kind of casual, satisfying lunch a rainy day calls for.
cards.why: Warm, filling, and totally no-fuss for families.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll or jog along this leafy creekside trail winding through Campbell — grab a flat, paved stretch and enjoy the canopy of oaks overhead, keeping an eye out for herons along the water.
cards.why: It's a gentle way to shake off lunch and stay close to the area before heading toward Mountain View.

cards.name: Apple at 50
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Swing through the Computer History Museum and check out the Apple at 50 exhibition. It's a visual, interactive look at how one company reshaped everything, and even younger kids get hooked by the old hardware and timeline displays.
cards.why: It's the kind of museum where kids ask questions instead of begging to leave.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by San Pedro Square Market and see what's going on.
cards.why: Mix and match lunch from a dozen vendors under one roof — tacos, ramen, banh mi, coffee. Best all-in-one food stop in downtown San Jose, especially on a warm weekend afternoon with a patio seat

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the day at Main Street Cupertino with dinner at Lazy Dog. The menu has something for everyone, the vibe is relaxed, and the kids' options are actually good. Grab a booth and decompress after a full day.
cards.why: Lazy Dog is reliable, family-friendly, and won't leave anyone at the table unhappy.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening browsing the upscale open-air shops, catching some live music near the fountains, or settling into one of the many bars and restaurants lining the Row.
cards.why: It's a lively but relaxed way to cap the night with good energy and plenty of options after Cupertino.

cards.name: Redwood Café by Chef Julien
cards.address: 450 Bryant St, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at Redwood Café by Chef Julien in Palo Alto. Grab a coffee and something from the menu before the midday rush hits.
cards.why: It's a genuinely great breakfast spot and the perfect low-key way to ease into the day.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Los Gatos Creek Trail for a morning walk along the creek. The trail is shaded and easy, good for a relaxed stroll without any serious elevation.
cards.why: With rain in the forecast later, getting outside in the morning is the smart call.

cards.name: Home Eat汉家宴 - San Jose
cards.address: 88 S 4th St STE 150, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Home Eat 汉家宴 in San Jose does homestyle Chinese cooking worth seeking out. Order generously and settle in for a proper sit-down lunch.
cards.why: A meal here feels like someone actually cooked for you, not just plated something.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Browse Santana Row in San Jose for a couple of hours. Walk the boulevard, duck into boutiques, and people-watch from one of the outdoor seating areas if the rain holds off.
cards.why: It's a solid rainy-day backup with covered walkways and plenty to do without committing to anything.

cards.name: Toby’s Board Game Club 一游未尽桌游吧
cards.address: 10805 N Wolfe Rd #106, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Toby's Board Game Club in Cupertino has a massive library of games and a laid-back atmosphere. Pick something from the shelves, grab a table, and actually play for a while.
cards.why: A rainy Monday afternoon is exactly when you want a cozy game bar to disappear into.

cards.name: tUrn Open Reading of Climate Plays: Informal Climate Play-Reading for actors and non-actors
cards.blurb: Catch the tUrn Open Reading of Climate Plays at Santa Clara University. It's an informal reading open to actors and non-actors alike, so just show up and participate or watch.
cards.why: Free, low-pressure, and genuinely interesting for a Monday evening.

cards.name: Clandestine Brewing
cards.address: 980 S 1st St ste b, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night at Clandestine Brewing in San Jose. Grab a pint or two from their tap list and decompress in a laid-back brewery atmosphere.
cards.why: A craft brewery is exactly the right ending to a day that already had a lot going on.

cards.name: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.address: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning on Castro Street grabbing breakfast bites and coffee from one of the cafes along the strip before the day gets going.
cards.why: It's a chill, walkable spot that's great for kids and gets you fueled up without any fuss.

cards.name: Algorithms of Love
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Dive into Algorithms of Love at the Computer History Museum. It's one of those exhibitions where the kids will actually stop and stare — interactive, visual, and genuinely cool for all ages.
cards.why: Rainy-day insurance and a legit interesting morning activity for the whole family.

cards.name: Homework Help for Grades K-6
cards.blurb: Are you a student in grades K-6th and need help with homework? We have tutors for you to receive homework help, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday from 4 p
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Main Street Cupertino for lunch. Lazy Dog is great with kids, or pick one of the Asian restaurants along the outdoor plaza for something a little different.
cards.why: The outdoor plaza is easy to navigate with kids and the restaurant options are solid across the board.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Vasona Lake County Park in Los Gatos for the afternoon. Walk the lake loop, let the kids run the open grass areas, and if the timing works, catch the little train that circles the park.
cards.why: Kids absolutely love the train and the lake views, and it burns off that post-lunch energy perfectly.

cards.name: Tong Sui Desserts & Drinks (Sunnyvale)
cards.address: 927 E Arques Ave #151, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Make a quick stop at Tong Sui Desserts in Sunnyvale for something sweet. Hong Kong-style dessert drinks and treats are a hit with kids, and the portions are generous.
cards.why: It's a fun, affordable treat that feels special without slowing the day down.

cards.name: Read Me
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Watch the program live on YouTube here! Whether youre an expert, student, or simply curious, how can you truly grasp what is happening in computing? One powerfu
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Philz Coffee
cards.address: 1976 S Bascom Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a custom pour at Philz — tell them how sweet and strong you want it and they'll dial it in cup by cup.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a rainy Monday morning is a great cup of coffee.

cards.name: Peralta Adobe & Fallon House
cards.address: Peralta Adobe, Downtown San Jose
cards.blurb: Walk through the oldest surviving building in San Jose and the adjacent Fallon House — two buildings, 200-plus years of history, and it's completely free.
cards.why: It's a drizzly morning and this is exactly the kind of indoor-outdoor spot that holds up in any weather.

cards.name: Philharmonia Baroque's Artist Salon--Shunske Sato at Martin Luther King Junior Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library for the Philharmonia Baroque Artist Salon featuring violinist Shunske Sato — it's a free, intimate midday performance worth catching.
cards.why: A live baroque salon on a rainy Monday afternoon is a pretty great way to spend your lunch hour.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market is a few blocks from King Library, so grab lunch from whatever vendor calls to you — tacos, banh mi, ramen, it's all under one roof with plenty of seating.
cards.why: After a sit-down event you want something casual and flexible, and this place delivers.

cards.name: AIDS: A People’s History of the Pandemic at King Library
cards.blurb: Loop back through King Library to take in the AIDS: A People's History of the Pandemic exhibition — it's a thoughtful, well-assembled show that deserves more than a quick walk-through.
cards.why: You're already downtown and this kind of exhibition rewards slowing down and actually reading.

cards.name: Social Stitching
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Social Stitching in Los Gatos is a drop-in community gathering at 110 E Main St — bring a project or just show up, meet some locals, and decompress.
cards.why: A chill, creative hangout is a perfect buffer between afternoon and dinner.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap up the night at Santana Row — browse the boulevard if the rain has let up, then settle in for dinner at Straits, Sino, or pull up a patio seat at Zinnia if it clears.
cards.why: Santana Row on a weeknight is relaxed and walkable, with solid dinner options without a long wait.

cards.name: Moonwake Coffee Roasters
cards.address: 1412 Saratoga Ave, San Jose, CA 95129, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with a proper cup at Moonwake Coffee Roasters. Grab a coffee for the adults and something cozy for the kids while everyone wakes up.
cards.why: Good coffee sets the tone for the whole day.

cards.name: Children's Discovery Museum
cards.address: Children's Discovery Museum of San Jose
cards.blurb: Two solid hours at the Children's Discovery Museum. Kids can splash at the WaterWays exhibit, crawl through the bubble zone, and go full chaos mode in the best possible way. It's built entirely for hands-on play so let them run.
cards.why: It's the best rainy-day spot in San Jose and the kids will talk about it on the drive home.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Head to San Pedro Square Market for lunch. A dozen vendors under one roof means everyone picks their own thing — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever. Grab a patio table if the weather cooperates.
cards.why: No fighting over where to eat when every option is right there.

cards.name: History Park at Kelley Park
cards.address: History Park at Kelley Park
cards.blurb: Take a lap through History Park at Kelley Park. There are historic buildings, a working trolley, and open space for kids to stretch their legs. It's free and genuinely interesting for the whole family.
cards.why: Free admission and fresh air after an indoor afternoon is the right move.

cards.name: frozo's
cards.address: 1000 Lafayette St D, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Frozo's in Santa Clara for a frozen treat before dinner. Fun flavors, low key vibe, and kids absolutely lose it for the place.
cards.why: Dessert before dinner is a family day privilege.

cards.name: Homework Help for Grades K-6
cards.blurb: Are you a student in grades K-6th and need help with homework? We have tutors for you to receive homework help, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday from 4 p
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Is a Liveable Planet a Human Right? The International Court of Justice weighs in
cards.blurb: Catch this morning talk at Santa Clara University on whether a liveable planet is a human right — the International Court of Justice recently weighed in and there's a lot to unpack.
cards.why: Free, thought-provoking, and a genuinely good way to kick off a Monday morning.

cards.name: Realign Therapy Massage Center
cards.address: 1053 E El Camino Real STE 4, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Treat yourself to a massage at Realign Therapy in Sunnyvale. A solid hour of bodywork mid-morning hits differently when you're not rushing back to anything.
cards.why: With rain in the forecast, this is exactly when you want to be inside getting worked on.

cards.name: Sustainable Systems Seminar Lunch Series - Quantifying Uncertainty in Urban Water Demand Projections
cards.address: 425 Santa Teresa St, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Head over to Stanford for this free lunch seminar on urban water demand projections. It's one of those talks that sounds niche but ends up being surprisingly gripping once they start showing the models.
cards.why: Free food, free ideas, and you'll walk out knowing something genuinely useful about how cities plan for water.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Spend a couple of hours at the Cantor Arts Center on campus. The Rodin sculpture garden is worth lingering in even on a grey day, and the indoor galleries rotate consistently strong work.
cards.why: You're already on Stanford's campus and this is one of the best free museums in the Bay Area.

cards.name: Sushi Boat
cards.address: 1026 W Evelyn Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94086, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Sushi Boat in Sunnyvale. The conveyor belt format makes it easy to graze at your own pace — grab whatever looks good as it comes around and order hot items off the menu.
cards.why: It's one of the most fun dinner formats in the South Bay and the fish is consistently fresh.

cards.name: Board of Library Trustees Meeting
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Drop into the Mountain View Board of Library Trustees meeting at 585 Franklin St. These public sessions are more interesting than they sound, especially when library funding or programming is on the agenda.
cards.why: It's a free, local civic experience and a genuinely different way to spend a Monday evening.

cards.name: 1 Oz Coffee
cards.address: 650 Castro St #130, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the morning with coffee and something to eat at this Castro Street spot — cozy enough to linger while everyone wakes up.
cards.why: Good coffee on a rainy morning sets the whole day up right.

cards.name: The Future of Humanoid Robots
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Walk through this exhibition and check out the latest in humanoid robots — the kids will be genuinely wide-eyed at what these machines can do.
cards.why: It's the kind of thing that sparks a hundred questions from curious kids.

cards.name: ZombieRunner Coffee
cards.address: 344 California Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by ZombieRunner Coffee and see what's going on.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Algorithms of Love
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Watch the program live on YouTube here! Online dating is a fast growing $9+ billion industry, and that’s just humans seeking humans! Dating AIs are already gene
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander through the Cantor's rotating galleries spanning ancient to contemporary art, then step outside to take in the Rodin sculptures scattered across the outdoor courtyard.
cards.why: It's a relaxed, covered way to spend a rainy afternoon right in Palo Alto.

cards.name: Siam Pavilion Thai Massage
cards.address: 830 Stewart Dr STE 172, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Settle into a Thai massage session to unknot the day's walking and standing before heading into the evening — they offer a range of pressure levels and session lengths.
cards.why: A mellow wind-down stop that bridges the afternoon and your dessert plans in Cupertino.

cards.name: MeloMelo Coconut Dessert
cards.address: 19652 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night with a bowl of MeloMelo coconut dessert — pick your toppings and let the kids go wild with the options.
cards.why: It's the perfect sweet ending and the kids will be talking about it the whole ride home.
